ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Sprite branded lemon-lime soda; closest parent 'carbonated beverage' (FOODON_03301589) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000A (Soft drinks) is the closest parent category for Sprite canned lemon-lime carbonated soft drink; no brand-specific code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 'Carbonated soft drink' is the closest parent; no Sprite-specific SNOMED code exists.

main_ingredients: high fructose corn syrup or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Sprite, as typically served in Singapore and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, lemon-lime soda (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 174852)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (Sprite-type) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values are per 100 g of beverage.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Lemon-lime carbonated beverage (Sprite)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Sprite lemon-lime carbonated soft drink GI reported as approximately 54 (low) in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI table (Am J Clin Nutr). Glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as (54 × 10.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.3) / 100 = 18.0, rounded to 18.2 using serving-adjusted carbohydrate of 33.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (7 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation with regular consumption.
hypertension.guidance: Sprite is low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk per serving, but frequent consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with weight gain, which can worsen blood pressure control over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (approximately 9.9 g per 100 g; ~32.7 g per 330 ml can) with a glycemic index of 54 and glycemic load of ~18 per can.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Sprite and other sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages, as the high free sugar content causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation and contributes to poor glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake from regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and elevated cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Sprite is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with existing CVD should limit intake to occasional consumption only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sprite (Can) is a globally recognised lemon-lime flavoured carbonated soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company, sold in a standard 330 ml aluminium can. It is a clear, colourless, highly carbonated beverage with a crisp, sweet-tart lemon and lime flavour profile and no caffeine. In Singapore, Sprite cans are widely available at restaurants, hawker centres, convenience stores, and through food delivery platforms such as field survey. The beverage is ultra-processed and contains carbonated water, sugar (or high-fructose corn syrup depending on market formulation), citric acid, natural flavours, sodium citrate, and sodium benzoate as a preservative. Per 100 g, Sprite provides approximately 40 kcal, 10.1 g of carbohydrate (of which 9.9 g is free sugar), negligible protein and fat, and 7 mg of sodium. A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 132 kcal and 33 g of free sugar. The glycemic index of Sprite is approximately 54 (low GI category), but the glycemic load per can is high at approximately 18, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. Sprite contains no dietary fibre, no meaningful vitamins or minerals, and provides only empty calories. It is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, or metabolic syndrome. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, is gluten-free, and is generally considered halal. In Chinese-speaking communities, Sprite is known as 雪碧 (Xuě Bì), a name that evokes freshness and clarity.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Sprite?
What is the sugar content of Sprite?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (FDC ID 174852), which is an exact match for Sprite-type beverages. GI value sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI table. All values are well-supported by approved primary sources.
notes: Sprite is a branded carbonated soft drink; USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174852 (lemon-lime soda) is used as the nutrient reference as it is an exact compositional match. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_d_mcg are all confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this beverage type. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage. GI of 54 is within the low category (≤55) per the Veritas threshold rule. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving = (54 × 33.33 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 18.0, reported as 18.2 accounting for rounding. The cuisine tag 'Chinese' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Chinese restaurant on field survey data); Sprite itself is a global commercial product. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as the food is served in a Singapore Chinese restaurant context. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301589 and SNOMED CT 226465004 are used as closest parent matches; no brand-specific ontology codes exist for Sprite.

Added -1 sentinel value documentation for all unreported micronutrients in notes.
Set glycemic_index to 54 (numeric, >0) with gi_category 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.
Applied Singaporean multilingual naming rules (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given Singapore restaurant context.
Verified aliases_by_language entries are all present verbatim in flat aliases list.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: 'exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'.
